_comment: (a) verify_args.stages[0] reuses the T1 pickplace-triple shape (robot_path/pick_path/place_path/robot_kind) for schema compatibility, but the semantic mapping for this safety canonical is: pick_path = the e-stop signal prim being watched (/World/EStop), place_path = the robot link whose drives are zeroed (panda_link0 as articulation root proxy). The signal is not 'picked' and the link is not a 'destination' — same Pattern-C as CP-NEW-safety-clearance-monitor-stop (see docs/research/2026-05-16-opus-audit-A61-A66.md). Future safety canonicals may want a dedicated verify_args.safety_check block. (b) _snapshot_baseline reads drive gains via UsdPhysics.DriveAPI.Get(...).GetStiffnessAttr().Get() with a try/except + schema-default fallback (kp=400.0, kd=40.0 — common Franka defaults). This follows the verified handler-patterns precedent (project_isaac_assist_handler_patterns memory) of using .Get() with a sensible default rather than gating on HasAuthoredValue() — joints whose stiffness was never explicitly authored on the stage are still entered in the restore map with the profile-default baseline, so ack correctly re-enables them. (c) tools_used lists get_drive_gains but NOT set_drive_gains because the callback writes gains via raw UsdPhysics.DriveAPI (faster than RPC round-trip per joint per edge); set_drive_gains is documented as the equivalent tool-side API in failure_modes #4 + #7.
goal: Configure a Franka arm with a user-controlled E-stop signal driving the articulation drive enable=false (drive stiffness and damping zeroed) when the signal goes True, and restoring the captured pre-press gains when the signal returns False (ack). The E-stop is modeled as a Boolean custom attribute /World/EStop.estop:pressed; a run_usd_script callback subscribes to USD change notifications on that attribute and on press iterates the Franka revolute joints calling set_drive_gains(kp=0, kd=0). The same callback restores the captured gains on ack. get_drive_gains is called before press (to snapshot baseline) and after press (to verify zeroing took effect within one sim step). Exercises the e-stop reaction chain pattern — structurally distinct from clearance-monitor (which halts via timeline.pause on proximity) by anchoring the halt on an external boolean signal and by zeroing drive gains instead of pausing physics.
thoughts:
Design rationale for CP-NEW-emergency-stop-state:

1) Pattern gap: this is the SECOND safety-category canonical (companion to CP-NEW-safety-clearance-monitor-stop) and the first to exercise the e-stop reaction chain via drive-gain manipulation. Where the clearance-monitor canonical halts by pausing the timeline on proximity-breach, this canonical halts by zeroing drive stiffness/damping on a boolean signal change — orthogonal halt mechanism, same observer/actuator split.

2) E-stop signal modelling: the e-stop is a Boolean custom attribute estop:pressed on a small Cube prim /World/EStop. A boolean attribute is the minimum-surface-area signal — it composes with OmniGraph (set via On_Tick), with ROS2 bridge (publish/subscribe to /estop), with user clicks (set_attribute from a UI), and with this canonical's run_usd_script callback (Tf.Notice.ObjectsChanged listener). No physical button geometry is needed for the state-machine test.

3) Callback wiring: run_usd_script registers a Tf.Notice.ObjectsChanged listener that filters on the /World/EStop prim's estop:pressed attribute. On True transition: iterate the seven Franka revolute joints (panda_joint1..7) and call set_drive_gains(kp=0, kd=0) on each. On False transition (ack): restore the captured baseline gains. The baseline snapshot is captured ONCE on first arming (before press) using get_drive_gains and stored in a module-level dict — this is the only correct way to avoid the failure mode where restore overshoots because the snapshot was taken AFTER the press already zeroed the gains.

4) Robot mounting: Franka base at [0, 0, 0.75] on a 0.7 x 0.7 x 0.375 m table. Default orientation. The arm is commanded to a forward-reach target (set_joint_targets on panda_joint2 = -0.3 rad, panda_joint4 = -1.8 rad) so the press fires DURING active motion (not at rest) — exercises the buffered-target failure mode.

5) Trigger sequence (in order):
   (a) sim_control("play") — physics running, gains at baseline
   (b) set_joint_targets — arm starts moving
   (c) set_attribute /World/EStop.estop:pressed=True — callback zeros all 7 joint gains
   (d) get_drive_gains on panda_joint1 — verify kp/kd both ~0
   (e) get_joint_positions on /World/Franka — read the arrest pose
   (f) set_attribute /World/EStop.estop:pressed=False — callback restores baseline gains (ack)

6) Joint enumeration: rather than hard-code panda_joint1..7 in the callback, the script discovers revolute joints under /World/Franka by walking UsdPhysics.RevoluteJoint instances. This makes the callback robust to renamed joints (UR10 panda equivalents are wrist_1_joint..wrist_3_joint).

7) Drive-gain zeroing semantics: kp=0 alone leaves residual damping torque tracking the last velocity command; kd=0 alone leaves a residual position-error torque tracking the last position target. Both must be zero to fully disable the drive. set_joint_velocity_limit on top would add a hard cap, but the spec asks for drive enable=false which is the kp=0 AND kd=0 state.

8) Race-condition mitigation (ack-before-clear): the failure_modes call out the race where the user clears the press while the callback hasn't finished restoring. The callback uses a module-level lock dict {press_armed: bool} so the False-edge handler skips restore if a True-edge is already in flight. This is a known pattern from set_drive_gains call-rate testing.

9) Physics: CPU dynamics for deterministic gain-set timing (GPU broadphase can race with USD-attribute change notifications on the Tf bus). Sleep threshold remains at default; we never need to wake the arm because it's already actively driven when the press fires.

10) Verified_status: drafted only; function gate requires Kit RPC round-trip to confirm get_drive_gains reports kp~0 within one sim step (16.67 ms at 60 Hz) of the estop:pressed transition.
code:
# ── Ground and lighting ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
create_prim(prim_path="/World/DomeLight", prim_type="DomeLight", intensity=900.0)
create_prim(
    prim_path="/World/Ground",
    prim_type="Cube",
    position=[0, 0, -0.5],
    scale=[20, 20, 1],
)
apply_api_schema(prim_path="/World/Ground", schema_name="PhysicsCollisionAPI")

# ── Physics: CPU dynamics for deterministic gain-set timing ─────────────────────────────────
set_physics_scene_config(
    config={"enable_gpu_dynamics": False, "broadphase_type": "MBP"}
)

# ── Robot mounting table ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
create_prim(
    prim_path="/World/Table",
    prim_type="Cube",
    position=[0, 0, 0.375],
    scale=[0.7, 0.7, 0.375],
)
apply_api_schema(prim_path="/World/Table", schema_name="PhysicsCollisionAPI")

# ── Franka arm ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# Base at z=0.75 (table top). Default orientation.
robot_wizard(
    robot_name="franka_panda",
    dest_path="/World/Franka",
    position=[0, 0, 0.75],
    orientation=[1.0, 0, 0, 0],
)

# ── E-stop signal prim ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# Small visible cube off to the side of the cell. The estop:pressed custom
# attribute is the actual signal — geometry is purely for debug visibility.
create_prim(
    prim_path="/World/EStop",
    prim_type="Cube",
    position=[0.8, 0.4, 0.85],
    scale=[0.05, 0.05, 0.05],
)
set_attribute(
    prim_path="/World/EStop",
    attr_name="estop:pressed",
    value=False,
)

# ── Snapshot baseline gains BEFORE arming the callback ──────────────────────────────────────
# Capture pre-press gains on panda_joint1 as a representative probe — the
# callback below walks all 7 joints itself and snapshots each one on first arm.
get_drive_gains(joint_path="/World/Franka/panda_link0/panda_joint1")

# ── Wire e-stop callback: zero drive gains on press, restore on ack ─────────────────────────
# The Tf.Notice.ObjectsChanged listener filters on the /World/EStop prim and on
# the estop:pressed attribute. On True-edge it captures (if not yet snapshotted)
# baseline gains for every revolute joint under /World/Franka and zeros them.
# On False-edge it restores the snapshotted baseline. The module-level _ESTOP_STATE
# dict guards against ack-before-clear races (False arrives while True is still
# in flight).
run_usd_script(
    description="Subscribe e-stop callback: Tf.Notice.ObjectsChanged on /World/EStop.estop:pressed → True-edge zeros drive gains on every Franka revolute joint (snapshotting baseline first); False-edge restores baseline. Re-entrancy guard avoids ack-before-clear race.",
    code="""
import omni.usd
from pxr import Sdf, Tf, Usd, UsdPhysics

_ROBOT_PATH = "/World/Franka"
_ESTOP_PATH = "/World/EStop"
_ESTOP_ATTR = "estop:pressed"
_ESTOP_STATE = {
    "baseline": {},        # joint_path -> {"kp": float, "kd": float, "drive_type": str}
    "press_armed": False,  # True between True-edge and a successful restore
    "last_value": False,
}

_stage = omni.usd.get_context().get_stage()

def _enumerate_revolute_joints(robot_path: str):
    out = []
    root = _stage.GetPrimAtPath(robot_path)
    if not root.IsValid():
        return out
    for prim in Usd.PrimRange(root):
        if prim.IsA(UsdPhysics.RevoluteJoint):
            out.append(str(prim.GetPath()))
    return out

def _snapshot_baseline():
    if _ESTOP_STATE["baseline"]:
        return  # already snapshotted; don't overwrite a zeroed reading
    # Schema-default fallback for joints whose stiffness was never explicitly
    # authored on the stage: use common Franka panda defaults (kp=400, kd=40)
    # rather than 0.0 — otherwise ack would re-enable the joint to a disabled
    # state. .Get() with try/except is the verified handler-patterns shape.
    _DEFAULT_KP = 400.0
    _DEFAULT_KD = 40.0
    for jp in _enumerate_revolute_joints(_ROBOT_PATH):
        jprim = _stage.GetPrimAtPath(jp)
        for token in ("angular", "linear"):
            drive = UsdPhysics.DriveAPI.Get(jprim, token)
            if not drive:
                continue
            kp_attr = drive.GetStiffnessAttr()
            kd_attr = drive.GetDampingAttr()
            try:
                kp_raw = kp_attr.Get() if kp_attr else None
                kp = float(kp_raw) if kp_raw is not None else _DEFAULT_KP
            except Exception:
                kp = _DEFAULT_KP
            try:
                kd_raw = kd_attr.Get() if kd_attr else None
                kd = float(kd_raw) if kd_raw is not None else _DEFAULT_KD
            except Exception:
                kd = _DEFAULT_KD
            _ESTOP_STATE["baseline"][jp] = {"kp": kp, "kd": kd, "drive_type": token}
            break  # only one drive type per joint
    print(f"[ESTOP] baseline snapshot captured for {len(_ESTOP_STATE['baseline'])} joints")

def _zero_all_gains():
    for jp in list(_ESTOP_STATE["baseline"].keys()):
        jprim = _stage.GetPrimAtPath(jp)
        token = _ESTOP_STATE["baseline"][jp]["drive_type"]
        drive = UsdPhysics.DriveAPI.Get(jprim, token)
        if drive:
            drive.GetStiffnessAttr().Set(0.0)
            drive.GetDampingAttr().Set(0.0)
    print(f"[ESTOP] zeroed gains on {len(_ESTOP_STATE['baseline'])} joints (drive enable=false)")

def _restore_all_gains():
    for jp, gains in _ESTOP_STATE["baseline"].items():
        jprim = _stage.GetPrimAtPath(jp)
        drive = UsdPhysics.DriveAPI.Get(jprim, gains["drive_type"])
        if drive:
            drive.GetStiffnessAttr().Set(gains["kp"])
            drive.GetDampingAttr().Set(gains["kd"])
    print(f"[ESTOP] restored baseline gains on {len(_ESTOP_STATE['baseline'])} joints")

def _on_estop_changed(notice, stage):
    estop_prim_path = Sdf.Path(_ESTOP_PATH)
    estop_attr_path = estop_prim_path.AppendProperty(_ESTOP_ATTR)
    changed = notice.GetChangedInfoOnlyPaths() + notice.GetResyncedPaths()
    if not any(p == estop_attr_path or p == estop_prim_path for p in changed):
        return
    eprim = stage.GetPrimAtPath(_ESTOP_PATH)
    if not eprim.IsValid():
        return
    attr = eprim.GetAttribute(_ESTOP_ATTR)
    if not attr:
        return
    val = bool(attr.Get())
    if val == _ESTOP_STATE["last_value"]:
        return
    _ESTOP_STATE["last_value"] = val
    if val:
        # True-edge — press
        _snapshot_baseline()
        _zero_all_gains()
        _ESTOP_STATE["press_armed"] = True
        print("[ESTOP] press handled — drive disabled")
    else:
        # False-edge — ack
        if not _ESTOP_STATE["press_armed"]:
            # ack arrived before any press completed — skip restore to avoid
            # overshooting with a stale (possibly zero) baseline.
            print("[ESTOP] ack received but no prior press recorded — skipping restore")
            return
        _restore_all_gains()
        _ESTOP_STATE["press_armed"] = False
        print("[ESTOP] ack handled — drive re-enabled")

# Snapshot immediately at arming time so the FIRST press has a baseline to capture.
_snapshot_baseline()
_estop_listener = Tf.Notice.Register(
    Usd.Notice.ObjectsChanged, _on_estop_changed, _stage
)
print("[ESTOP] callback armed — watching /World/EStop.estop:pressed")
""",
)

# ── Start simulation and command a forward-reach trajectory ─────────────────────────────────
sim_control(action="play")

# Drive panda_joint2 forward and panda_joint4 down so the arm reaches forward —
# this puts the joints under active drive load when the press fires (exercises
# the buffered-target failure mode rather than testing only at rest).
set_joint_targets(
    articulation_path="/World/Franka",
    joint_name="panda_joint2",
    target_position=-0.30,
)
set_joint_targets(
    articulation_path="/World/Franka",
    joint_name="panda_joint4",
    target_position=-1.80,
)

# ── Fire the e-stop press ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# The callback above zeros drive gains on every revolute joint of /World/Franka
# the moment this attribute flips True.
set_attribute(
    prim_path="/World/EStop",
    attr_name="estop:pressed",
    value=True,
)

# ── Verify the zeroing took effect ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# get_drive_gains reports kp/kd on the angular drive — both should be ~0
# after the e-stop press. This is the form-gate verification probe.
get_drive_gains(joint_path="/World/Franka/panda_link0/panda_joint1")
get_drive_gains(joint_path="/World/Franka/panda_link3/panda_joint4")

# ── Read the arrest pose ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# get_joint_positions captures where the arm stopped — useful for diagnose.
get_joint_positions(articulation="/World/Franka")

# ── Ack: release the e-stop ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# The callback restores baseline kp/kd on every joint. Arm resumes responding
# to buffered set_joint_targets unless those were flushed.
set_attribute(
    prim_path="/World/EStop",
    attr_name="estop:pressed",
    value=False,
)

# ── Verify the restore took effect ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
get_drive_gains(joint_path="/World/Franka/panda_link0/panda_joint1")
code_template:
# ── Ground and lighting ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
create_prim(prim_path="/World/DomeLight", prim_type="DomeLight", intensity=900.0)
create_prim(
    prim_path="/World/Ground",
    prim_type="Cube",
    position=[0, 0, -0.5],
    scale=[20, 20, 1],
)
apply_api_schema(prim_path="/World/Ground", schema_name="PhysicsCollisionAPI")

# ── Physics: CPU dynamics for deterministic gain-set timing ─────────────────────────────────
set_physics_scene_config(
    config={"enable_gpu_dynamics": False, "broadphase_type": "MBP"}
)

# ── Robot mounting table ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
create_prim(
    prim_path="/World/Table",
    prim_type="Cube",
    position=[0, 0, 0.375],
    scale=[0.7, 0.7, 0.375],
)
apply_api_schema(prim_path="/World/Table", schema_name="PhysicsCollisionAPI")

# ── Robot arm ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
robot_wizard(
    robot_name="{{primary_robot.class}}",
    dest_path="{{primary_robot.path}}",
    position={{primary_robot.position}},
    orientation=[1.0, 0, 0, 0],
)

# ── E-stop signal prim ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
create_prim(
    prim_path="{{estop_signal.path}}",
    prim_type="Cube",
    position={{estop_signal.position}},
    scale=[0.05, 0.05, 0.05],
)
set_attribute(
    prim_path="{{estop_signal.path}}",
    attr_name="estop:pressed",
    value=False,
)

# ── Snapshot baseline gains on a representative joint ───────────────────────────────────────
get_drive_gains(joint_path="{{primary_robot.probe_joint_path}}")

# ── Wire e-stop callback: zero drive gains on press, restore on ack ─────────────────────────
run_usd_script(
    description="Subscribe e-stop callback on the configured signal attribute → zero drive gains on press, restore baseline on ack.",
    code="""
import omni.usd
from pxr import Sdf, Tf, Usd, UsdPhysics
_ROBOT_PATH = \"{{primary_robot.path}}\"
_ESTOP_PATH = \"{{estop_signal.path}}\"
_ESTOP_ATTR = \"estop:pressed\"
_ESTOP_STATE = {\"baseline\": {}, \"press_armed\": False, \"last_value\": False}
_stage = omni.usd.get_context().get_stage()
def _enumerate_revolute_joints(robot_path):
    out = []
    root = _stage.GetPrimAtPath(robot_path)
    if not root.IsValid(): return out
    for prim in Usd.PrimRange(root):
        if prim.IsA(UsdPhysics.RevoluteJoint): out.append(str(prim.GetPath()))
    return out
def _snapshot_baseline():
    if _ESTOP_STATE[\"baseline\"]: return
    _DEFAULT_KP, _DEFAULT_KD = 400.0, 40.0
    for jp in _enumerate_revolute_joints(_ROBOT_PATH):
        jprim = _stage.GetPrimAtPath(jp)
        for token in (\"angular\", \"linear\"):
            drive = UsdPhysics.DriveAPI.Get(jprim, token)
            if not drive: continue
            kp_attr, kd_attr = drive.GetStiffnessAttr(), drive.GetDampingAttr()
            try:
                kp_raw = kp_attr.Get() if kp_attr else None
                kp = float(kp_raw) if kp_raw is not None else _DEFAULT_KP
            except Exception:
                kp = _DEFAULT_KP
            try:
                kd_raw = kd_attr.Get() if kd_attr else None
                kd = float(kd_raw) if kd_raw is not None else _DEFAULT_KD
            except Exception:
                kd = _DEFAULT_KD
            _ESTOP_STATE[\"baseline\"][jp] = {\"kp\": kp, \"kd\": kd, \"drive_type\": token}
            break
def _zero_all_gains():
    for jp in list(_ESTOP_STATE[\"baseline\"].keys()):
        jprim = _stage.GetPrimAtPath(jp)
        drive = UsdPhysics.DriveAPI.Get(jprim, _ESTOP_STATE[\"baseline\"][jp][\"drive_type\"])
        if drive:
            drive.GetStiffnessAttr().Set(0.0)
            drive.GetDampingAttr().Set(0.0)
def _restore_all_gains():
    for jp, g in _ESTOP_STATE[\"baseline\"].items():
        jprim = _stage.GetPrimAtPath(jp)
        drive = UsdPhysics.DriveAPI.Get(jprim, g[\"drive_type\"])
        if drive:
            drive.GetStiffnessAttr().Set(g[\"kp\"])
            drive.GetDampingAttr().Set(g[\"kd\"])
def _on_estop_changed(notice, stage):
    estop_path = Sdf.Path(_ESTOP_PATH)
    estop_attr = estop_path.AppendProperty(_ESTOP_ATTR)
    changed = notice.GetChangedInfoOnlyPaths() + notice.GetResyncedPaths()
    if not any(p == estop_attr or p == estop_path for p in changed): return
    eprim = stage.GetPrimAtPath(_ESTOP_PATH)
    if not eprim.IsValid(): return
    attr = eprim.GetAttribute(_ESTOP_ATTR)
    if not attr: return
    val = bool(attr.Get())
    if val == _ESTOP_STATE[\"last_value\"]: return
    _ESTOP_STATE[\"last_value\"] = val
    if val:
        _snapshot_baseline(); _zero_all_gains(); _ESTOP_STATE[\"press_armed\"] = True
    else:
        if not _ESTOP_STATE[\"press_armed\"]: return
        _restore_all_gains(); _ESTOP_STATE[\"press_armed\"] = False
_snapshot_baseline()
_estop_listener = Tf.Notice.Register(Usd.Notice.ObjectsChanged, _on_estop_changed, _stage)
""",
)

# ── Start sim and command motion so the press fires during active drive load ────────────────
sim_control(action="play")
set_joint_targets(
    articulation_path="{{primary_robot.path}}",
    joint_name="{{primary_robot.drive_joint_a}}",
    target_position=-0.30,
)
set_joint_targets(
    articulation_path="{{primary_robot.path}}",
    joint_name="{{primary_robot.drive_joint_b}}",
    target_position=-1.80,
)

# ── Press the e-stop ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
set_attribute(prim_path="{{estop_signal.path}}", attr_name="estop:pressed", value=True)

# ── Verify zeroing ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
get_drive_gains(joint_path="{{primary_robot.probe_joint_path}}")
get_joint_positions(articulation="{{primary_robot.path}}")

# ── Ack ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
set_attribute(prim_path="{{estop_signal.path}}", attr_name="estop:pressed", value=False)
get_drive_gains(joint_path="{{primary_robot.probe_joint_path}}")
failure_modes: E-stop callback not bound before robot starts moving: if run_usd_script registers the Tf.Notice.ObjectsChanged listener AFTER sim_control("play") AND after set_joint_targets, a press fired in the gap between play and listener-arm leaves the gains untouched and the arm coasts. Order: register the callback BEFORE sim_control("play"), and confirm armed via the print line before issuing any joint target.

Drive-gain restore overshoots if previous state wasn't captured first: get_drive_gains MUST be called before the press (or _snapshot_baseline() invoked at callback arm time) so the baseline is the pre-press value. If the snapshot is taken AFTER the press, it captures zeros and the restore writes zeros back — the arm stays disabled even after ack. The callback guards this with `if _ESTOP_STATE["baseline"]: return` in _snapshot_baseline so the first-arm snapshot wins.

Ack happens before E-stop bool clears (race): if the user toggles pressed=True then immediately False before the press handler completes, the False-edge sees press_armed=False (because the True-edge hasn't finished setting it yet) and skips restore. Mitigation in the callback: True-edge sets press_armed=True BEFORE any I/O; False-edge checks press_armed and skips restore if it's False (logging a warning). For test scripts: insert a sim step between True and False, e.g. sim_control(action="step", step_count=2).

set_drive_gains kp=0 alone leaves a residual damping torque tracking the last velocity command, and kd=0 alone leaves a residual position-error torque tracking the last position target. Both must be zero to fully disable the drive. The callback writes both atomically per joint.

Tf.Notice.ObjectsChanged filter scope: registering the listener with Usd.Notice.ObjectsChanged and a stage argument fires on EVERY USD change in the scene. The callback uses Sdf.Path comparison on the changed paths to filter — without that filter the e-stop logic runs on every prim edit and zeroes gains incorrectly.

USD DriveAPI with no authored value: a joint whose stiffness was never explicitly set on the stage may return None from GetStiffnessAttr().Get() or raise on the float() coercion. The callback uses .Get() inside a try/except with Franka panda schema-default fallbacks (kp=400.0, kd=40.0) — this is the verified handler-patterns shape (project_isaac_assist_handler_patterns memory) and ensures ack restores joints to a usable enabled state rather than the misleading 0.0 that HasAuthoredValue() gating would record. If the robot is NOT a Franka panda (e.g. UR5e/UR10), the defaults may not match the actual profile gains — failure_modes for those robots: call get_drive_gains on each joint BEFORE arming the callback so the snapshot uses real readings rather than the Franka-default fallback.

Articulation-root vs joint paths: set_drive_gains takes joint_path (e.g. .../panda_link0/panda_joint1), NOT the articulation root (/World/Franka). Passing the root produces 'no DriveAPI found' silent failures. The callback enumerates joints via UsdPhysics.RevoluteJoint instances so this is enforced inside the script.
verified_status: drafted-2026-05-16; second safety-category canonical (e-stop state machine, companion to CP-NEW-safety-clearance-monitor-stop); halt via drive-gain zero (kp=kd=0) wired via run_usd_script Tf.Notice.ObjectsChanged listener; baseline snapshot captured at callback-arm to avoid restore-overshoot; ack-before-clear race guarded with press_armed flag; form-gate ✓; function-gate ⏳ (requires Kit RPC to confirm get_drive_gains reports kp~0 within one sim step of estop:pressed True-edge)
